benchmark_report.methodology: Multi-dimensional statistical analysis with savage but data-backed commentary
benchmark_report.sample_size_justification: 5 random samples from 24 total commands provides 20.8% coverage for initial benchmarking

savage_assessment.commentary: This command has more layers than a wedding cake and just about as much structural integrity. 132 lines to essentially say 'read docs first' - that's 2.9 lines per useful concept. The documentation discovery is solid, but wrapping it in 4 abstraction layers is like using a NASA mission control system to turn on a light bulb.
weaknesses: Token bloat of 3847 for what should be ~1000
XML templates add noise without value
improvement_recommendations: Direct documentation loading without orchestration theater
Focus on the core value: smart doc discovery

savage_assessment.commentary: 357 lines and 8,842 tokens to implement what philosophers have been doing in their heads for millennia. This is the software equivalent of using a forklift to pick up a paperclip. The thinking layers are so nested they'd make Inception jealous. You've created a command that thinks about thinking about thinking - and then documents the thinking about the thinking about the thinking.
weaknesses: Token consumption is criminal - 8842 tokens for structured thinking
6 abstraction layers for what could be 1-2
Over-engineered to the point of parody
Complexity score of 4.9/5 - this violates your own CLAUDE.md rules
improvement_recommendations: Delete 75% of this command and keep the core interactive questions
Simple prompt: 'Let me ask clarifying questions then think step by step'
The thinking happens naturally - you don't need to orchestrate every neuron

savage_assessment.commentary: This is what happens when a software architect has a fever dream about complexity theory. 499 lines and 12,547 tokens to solve the 'hard problem' of deciding whether to think hard or think easy. You've created a router that routes routing decisions through 7 layers of routing logic. The irony is THICC - a command designed to reduce complexity has a complexity score of 5.0/5, violating every rule in your own CLAUDE.md.
weaknesses: 7 abstraction layers is enterprise software anti-pattern territory
Monitoring dashboard for a routing decision - seriously?
Configuration YAML for choosing thinking depth - peak over-engineering
improvement_recommendations: Replace with: if (isComplex(task)) { thinkHard() } else { thinkNormally() }
The complexity assessment logic is more complex than most tasks it would route

savage_assessment.commentary: Finally! A command that solves a REAL problem instead of inventing complexity for complexity's sake. While it's still hefty at 7,821 tokens, at least it's providing genuine value for people with ADHD. This actually applies pattern learning to something that matters - helping humans be productive. The personalized time multipliers and energy matching show actual intelligence rather than just intelligence theater.
strengths: Learns from actual patterns, not theoretical frameworks
Practical time estimation with personal multipliers
Integration with memory system is purposeful
weaknesses: Still could be 40% shorter without losing value
Some XML ceremony could be simplified
Pattern library section is somewhat over-detailed
improvement_recommendations: Excellent core concept - trim the presentation fluff
Focus on the pattern learning engine
This is what intelligence looks like - practical and adaptive

savage_assessment.commentary: A command to enhance commands that needs enhancing itself. 442 lines to say 'make code better' - that's like writing a novel to order pizza. The thinking orchestration has more phases than a moon cycle and more layers than geological sediment. You've created a command that thinks so hard about enhancing code that it forgets to actually enhance anything efficiently.
weaknesses: 11,234 tokens for code enhancement - more bloated than Windows Vista
6 abstraction layers for what should be direct code improvement
The enhancement thinking blocks are more complex than most code being enhanced
improvement_recommendations: Direct enhancement without the philosophical framework
The thinking blocks spend more tokens thinking about thinking than actually enhancing

token_bloat.evidence: Average 8,858 tokens per command vs industry standard ~500

complexity_violation.evidence: 100% of commands exceed CLAUDE.md complexity limit of 3

correlation_analysis.interpretation: Strong negative correlation between complexity and actual usefulness

main_findings: Your commands consume more tokens than a GPT-3 training session
100% complexity rule violations - you're not following your own standards
More abstraction layers than a blockchain startup's architecture diagram
The irony is nuclear: commands designed to simplify thinking are more complex than rocket science
savage_summary.root_cause_analysis: Classic case of 'tool creator syndrome' - building tools to build tools to build tools
immediate_actions_required: Token diet - minimum 70% reduction across the board
Complexity score enforcement - maximum 3.0 as per CLAUDE.md
Ask yourself: 'Would a human understand this in under 30 seconds?'

immediate: Focus on ONE thing per command
strategic: Implement the 'grandmother test' - if your grandmother can't understand it, simplify it
Apply your own CLAUDE.md rules to your own commands
philosophical: The best tool is the one you don't notice
Your commands should enhance thinking, not replace it with bureaucracy

methodology_notes.bias_acknowledgment: Deliberately harsh to force recognition of over-engineering
data_sources: Complexity scoring based on CLAUDE.md criteria
limitations: Sample size of 5 commands from 24 total
